title.rendered: Open Access and the Transformation of Scholarly Communication

metadata.pb_title: Open Access and the Transformation of Scholarly Communication
metadata.pb_description: This book explores the open access movement and its implications for scholarly communication, libraries, and research dissemination. Covers major policy frameworks, licensing models, and institutional repository strategies.
metadata.pb_keywords_tags: open access, scholarly communication, libraries, copyright

title.rendered: Introduction to Statistics for the Social Sciences

metadata.pb_title: Introduction to Statistics for the Social Sciences
metadata.pb_description: A practical introduction to statistical methods used in the social sciences. Topics include descriptive statistics, probability, hypothesis testing, correlation, and regression, with real-world social science examples throughout.
metadata.pb_keywords_tags: statistics, social sciences, research methods, quantitative

title.rendered: Technical Writing for Engineers and Scientists

metadata.pb_title: Technical Writing for Engineers and Scientists
metadata.pb_description: A comprehensive guide to technical writing for engineering and science students. Covers reports, proposals, instructions, and presentations, with emphasis on clarity, precision, and audience awareness.
metadata.pb_keywords_tags: technical writing, engineering, communication, professional writing

title.rendered: Principles of Psychology: A Bilingual Approach

metadata.pb_title: Principles of Psychology: A Bilingual Approach
metadata.pb_description: An introductory psychology textbook developed at the University of Rochester. Covers sensation and perception, learning and memory, cognition, development, personality, social psychology, and clinical psychology.
metadata.pb_keywords_tags: psychology, introductory, mental health, cognition, behavior

title.rendered: Principles of Economics: Microeconomics and Macroeconomics

metadata.pb_title: Principles of Economics: Microeconomics and Macroeconomics
metadata.pb_authors: N. Gregory Mankiw; Ronald D. Kneebone; Kenneth J. McKenzie
metadata.pb_description: A combined introduction to microeconomics and macroeconomics developed for students at the University of Rochester. Uses real-world examples from the Rochester metropolitan area and Western New York.

metadata.pb_authors: Department of Chemistry, University of Rochester
metadata.pb_description: A laboratory manual for the two-semester general chemistry sequence at the University of Rochester. Includes pre-lab questions, safety procedures, data collection sheets, and post-lab analysis for each experiment.

An introductory textbook covering computer fundamentals, operating systems, productivity software, internet navigation, digital citizenship, and basic cybersecurity. Designed for students with little or no prior computer experience.
metadata.pb_keywords_tags: computers, technology, digital literacy, internet, introductory

metadata.pb_editors: Jennifer Lapum; Oona St-Amant; Bruce Jakubec
metadata.pb_description: A comprehensive textbook for nursing students covering the fundamental concepts and skills needed to practice professional nursing. Topics include patient assessment, medication administration, infection control, and therapeutic communication.
metadata.pb_keywords_tags: nursing, healthcare, patient care, clinical skills, health sciences
